symptoms.symptom_name: Painful monocular visual blurring, worse on eye movement
symptoms.character: central blur with a dull retro-orbital ache aggravated by eye movement

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Pain worse on eye movement with monocular central visual loss is characteristic of optic neuritis (inflamed nerve tethered at the orbital apex).
Cortical/retinal ischaemic visual loss is typically painless and often homonymous or altitudinal, not painful and monocular-central.
A second sensory symptom concurrent with optic symptoms suggests more than one CNS focus, consistent with demyelination.
Bell's palsy is a facial MOTOR weakness; isolated facial sensory tingling without droop does not fit.

symptoms.symptom_name: Subacute onset over 1-2 hours (not instantaneous maximal deficit)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Demyelinating deficits typically evolve over hours to days rather than reaching maximum instantly.
Ischaemic deficits are often maximal at onset, though stroke can still fluctuate — does not exclude it.

symptoms.symptom_name: No limb weakness, dysarthria, facial droop or gait disturbance (patient-reported)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of FAST features lowers the probability of a large-vessel cortical stroke but does NOT exclude it without examination and imaging.

symptoms.symptom_name: No severe headache, nausea, photophobia or flashing-light aura

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of headache, nausea, photophobia and positive visual phenomena makes migrainous aura less likely.
A RAPD localises the lesion to the afferent (optic nerve) pathway — a hallmark of optic neuritis. Sealed: requires clinician examination, unobtainable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Reduced visual acuity in the right eye vs left

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objectively reduced monocular acuity supports an optic nerve lesion. Sealed: requires formal acuity testing.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal limb power (5/5), normal gait and coordination, no facial droop on examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal motor, gait and cranial-nerve examination argues strongly against an acute motor stroke. Sealed: requires clinician examination — the exact step telehealth cannot perform.

symptoms.symptom_name: Facial sensation intact to light touch despite subjective tingling

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Subjective paraesthesia with intact objective sensation is common in demyelination. Sealed clinician finding.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal vital signs (BP 122/78, HR 80, afebrile, SpO2 99%)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal vitals neither confirm nor exclude stroke. Sealed clinician measurement — the patient did not offer home-device readings in this encounter.

symptoms.symptom_name: MRI brain and orbits showing limited demyelination

investigation_details.investigation_name: MRI brain and orbits with contrast
investigation_details.expected_result: T2/FLAIR hyperintensity and gadolinium enhancement of the right optic nerve; limited demyelinating lesions; assess dissemination in space
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Demyelination and MS-risk stratification are missed and stroke is not formally excluded

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Clinically isolated syndrome (optic neuritis) — first demyelinating event
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: MRI demonstrating optic nerve demyelination confirms the diagnosis and stratifies MS risk. Sealed: hospital/outpatient imaging, never available in real time via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Together these strongly localise a lesion to the right optic nerve and are the clinical core of optic neuritis.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent non-contrast CT brain (in the emergency assessment) to exclude acute haemorrhage/infarct
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: First-line emergency neuroimaging to exclude haemorrhage and gross infarct in a patient with acute focal deficit before any stroke-specific treatment is considered.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: MRI brain and orbits with contrast
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Definitive imaging to confirm optic nerve demyelination and assess dissemination in space for MS-risk stratification.
First demyelinating event requires specialist assessment for diagnosis confirmation, steroid decision and follow-up.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Formal visual acuity and ophthalmology assessment
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Documents baseline acuity, confirms RAPD and monitors recovery of the affected eye.
Not appropriate on a telehealth basis and not indicated here — stroke-specific treatment follows in-person assessment and imaging, not a remote presumption.
